title: Integration tests — archive-hint snapshot + E3 self-trigger e2e + Phase 0.4 gate branch
description: Three new integration test surfaces: (1) archive-hint stdout snapshot — verify the new bilingual wording renders correctly for both zh-CN and en variants under various event ledger states (with session_id, without session_id, rotation cutoff); (2) end-to-end Phase 0.4 trigger gate — verify the gate skip happens for E1/E3/E5 contexts vs executes for E2 (via mocked SKILL.md entry context detection); (3) outcome event emission — verify Phase 2.5 writes session_archive_attempted with correct outcome enum for each terminal state.

files.change: Create with 6 test cases per TASK-03 spec — snapshot the stdout JSON for 6 scenarios. Use Vitest snapshot per existing precedent in __snapshots__/.

Create 5 test cases — one per entry_point (E1/E2/E3/E4/E5) — verify Phase 0.4 onboard prompt fires only for E2/E4 and skips for E1/E3/E5. Mock by writing a temp SKILL.md invocation and asserting on expected event-emission patterns vs AskUserQuestion-fired markers.

Create 4 test cases mapping skill terminal states to outcome enum: (1) successful Phase 2 with N candidates → outcome='proposed' + correct knowledge_proposed_ids; (2) Phase 0.5 gate FAIL → outcome='skipped_no_signal'; (3) User dismisses all candidates → outcome='user_dismissed'; (4) Multi-session archive emits one event per session.

implementation: Create archive-hint-copy.test.ts: stub fs to provide hand-crafted events.jsonl content, invoke archive-hint.cjs as a child process, snapshot stdout JSON.
For test case 1 (session_id present on most events), use events with session_id field populated; assert reason contains '跨 N 个会话'.
For test case 2 (session_id missing), assert reason contains '跨多个会话累计'.
For test case 3 (rotation cutoff, no knowledge_proposed event), assert reason contains 'watermark 已被 rotation 清理'.
For test case 4-5 (zh-CN / en variants), control via fabric-config.json fixture.
For test case 6 (cooldown regression), use a writeShownCache fixture from existing test pattern.

Create archive-skill-trigger-gate.test.ts: this is harder since SKILL.md is markdown-driven AI behavior. Approach: spawn a Claude Code session, invoke /fabric-archive with controlled prompts simulating each entry_point, parse stdout for the expected Phase 0.4 marker. If too brittle, REPLACE with a unit test that validates the SKILL.md document itself contains the required gate logic (grep + parse the markdown).
Create archive-attempt-outcomes.test.ts: integration test the appendEventLedgerEvent path with mocked Phase 2 outcomes. Verify event-ledger.ts schema validation passes for all 4 outcome enums.
Run pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test, pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test; all green.

reference.pattern: Existing integration tests at packages/cli/__tests__/integration/* and packages/server/__tests__/* — same Vitest + snapshot conventions
reference.examples: rc.22 integration tests for hooks + rc.24 cite contract integration tests are the structural precedent.

rationale.chosen_approach: Three focused integration tests covering the three highest-risk surfaces. Skip exhaustive unit tests for SKILL.md changes (those are markdown docs, validated by manual dogfood in TASK-12).
alternatives_considered: Manual dogfood only (rejected: regression risk too high — rc.25 changes 8+ files)
Full e2e Claude Code spawn for every entry_point (rejected: too brittle and slow)
Skip integration entirely (rejected: TASK-12 dogfood alone isn't reproducible for CI)
decision_factors: Snapshot tests for archive-hint wording catch regressions cheaply
Gate-branch tests can fall back to SKILL.md grep validation if spawn is brittle
Outcome enum tests are pure server-side and easy to mock
rationale.tradeoffs: Gate-branch test reliability depends on whether we can spawn a Claude Code session in CI; mitigation: degrade to SKILL.md grep validation.

criteria: All 3 test files have ≥ described count of cases (6 + 5 + 4)
Commit msg: 'test(rc25): integration tests for hint + trigger gate + outcomes (TASK-11)'
